docs.title: byconaut
docs.text:
The byconaut package contains scripts for data processing for and based on the bycon package. The main use cases are:

  • generation of utility collections for the standard Progenetix data model
    • collations
    • frequencymaps provide binned CNV frequency values for samples belonging to a given collation code
  • I/O & transformations for bycon generated files

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
byconaut depends on the bycon package which can be downloaded from its repository. Please see the repository and the corresponding documentation site.

While there is also a pip installation possible over pip3 install bycon this will not include the local configuration files necessary e.g. for processing the databases.

docs.title:
Option A: examplez from
  1. download
  2. unpack somewhere & restore with (your paths etc.): 
    mongosh examplez --eval 'db.dropDatabase()'
mongorestore --db $database .../mongodump/examplez/
  3. proceed w/ step 4 ... below

Option B: Create your own databases
  1. Create database and variants collection
  2. update the local bycon installation for your database information andlocal parameters
    • database name(s)
    • filter_definitions for parameter mapping
  3. Create metadata collections - analyses, biosamples and individuals
  4. Create statusmaps and CNV statistics for the analyses collection
    • only relevant for CNV database use cases
  5. Create the collations collection which uses filter_definitions and the corresponding values to aggregate information for query matching, term expansion ...
  6. Create frequencymaps for binned CNV data
    • relies on existence of statusmaps in analyses and collations
    • only needed for CNV data
Data maintenance scripts
docs.text: Please see the helper apps documentation.

docs.title: Helper Applications
docs.text:
The byconaut repository provides a number of helper applications with different types of functionalities, e.g.

  • data I/O
  • plotting (see plotting)
  • database maintenance
  • data transformation

These applications are in some way used to populate or manage data resources for bycon driven implementations of the Beacon protokol (i.e. genomic data resources).

docs.title: Plotting Apps
docs.text: For more information see the dedicated documentation page).

docs.title: analysesStatusmapsRefresher
docs.text: This is one of the housekeeping scripts which has to be run after CNV data has been added or modified in the database. It creates CNV status data for binned genome intervals, used for histogram generation, sample clustering etc., as well as some other statistics (e.g. CNV coverage per chromosomal arms ...).

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text:
• -d, --datasetIds ... to select the dataset (only one per run)
  • --filters ... to (optionally) limit the processing to a subset of samples (e.g. after a limited update)

docs.title: Use
docs.text:
• bin/analysesStatusmapsRefresher.py -d progenetix
  • bin/analysesStatusmapsRefresher.py -d progenetix --filters "pgx:icdom-81703"
  • bin/analysesStatusmapsRefresher.py -d cellz --filters "cellosaurus:CVCL_0312"

docs.title: collationsCreator
docs.text:
The collationsCreator script updates the dataset specific collations collections which provide the aggregated data (sample numbers, hierarchy trees etc.) for all individual codes belonging to one of the entities defined in the filter_definitions in the bycon configuration. The (optional) hierarchy data is provided in rsrc/classificationTrees/__filterType__/numbered-hierarchies.tsv as a list of ordered branches in the format code | label | depth | order.

TBD The filter definition should be one of the configuration where users can provide additions and overrides in the byconaut/local directory.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text:
• -d, --datasetIds ... to select the dataset (only one per run)
  • --filters ... to (optionally) limit the processing to a subset of samples (e.g. after a limited update)

docs.title: Use
docs.text:
• bin/collationsCreator.py -d progenetix
  • bin/collationsCreator.py -d examplez --collationTypes "PMID"

docs.title: frequencymapsCreator
docs.text: This app creates the frequency maps for the "collations" collection. Basically, all samples matching any of the collation codes and representing CNV analyses are selected and the frequencies of CNVs per genomic bin are aggregated. The result contains teh gain and loss frquencies for all genomic intervals, for the given entity.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text:
• -d, --datasetIds ... to select the dataset (only one per run)
  • --collationTypes ... to (optionally) limit the processing to a selected collation types (e.g. NCIT, PMID, icdom ...)

docs.title: Use
docs.text:
• bin/frequencymapsCreator.py -d progenetix
  • bin/frequencymapsCreator.py -d examplez --collationTypes "icdot"

docs.title: ISCNsegmenter
docs.text: This is a helper app to transform cytogenetic CGH annotations (rev ish) to the canonical tab-delimited .pgxseg segment file format.

docs.title: Use
docs.text: • bin/ISCNsegmenter.py -i imports/ccghtest.tab -o exports/cghtest-with-histo.pgxseg

docs.title: Changes & TODOs
docs.text: Changes are now documented in bycon. This page may lists some very specific byconaut changes and plans.
• added the _byconServicesDB (genes, geolocs, geocountries) to the /rsrc/mongodump/ directory

• moved MongoDB index generation to housekeeping.py
  • started to organize actions in housekeeping.py w/ user prompts

docs.title: byconaut Plot functionality
docs.text: Starting with version v1.0.30 (2023-04-14) the bycon package added the ability to produce the typical Progenetix-style CNV histograms and CNV sample plots, later (bycon v.1.3.4) moved to the byconaut repository.

docs.title: Plotting services
docs.text:
Plotting "services" are online service endpoints for generating visualizations of mostly CNV data from the databases of the respective beaconized resources. In the case of the developers these would be e.g. progenetix.org and cancercelllines.org whcich also are being used for the active examples below. The plotting services - which are maintained inside byconaut/services but installed in the corresponding webserver CGI directory - are:

  • services/collationplots/
  • services/sampleplots/

docs.title: Plotting Applications
docs.text:
Plotting "applications" provide command line utilities for the plotting of database content and local files. These are maintained in the byconaut/bin directory:

  • bin/collationsPlotter.py
  • bin/samplesPlotter.py
  • bin/pgxsegPlotter.py

docs.title: Plotting Functionality
docs.text:
Plots can now be generated:

  • for samples and aggregated data using standard Beacon v2 query parameters and filters, through special /services/... endpoints
    • /services/collationplots for pre-computed frequency histograms and heatstrips
    • /services/sampleplots for query-derived sample plots (including histograms)
      • plot selection through plotType= with options histoplot, samplesplot and histoheatplot
      • defaults to histoplot if unspecified
  • for files uploaded through the Progenetix Upload... interface (not terribly stable...)
  • through commabnd line scripts in this project's bin directory, e.g. for provided .pgxseg ... files

Custom syntax for plot parameters

To limit the amount of pre-defined parameters accepted through the bycon interface we are using a special syntax for plot parameters. Plot parameters (see below for all pre-defined ones) are provided as a single string to plotPars parameter, with individual parameter pairs concatenated by :: * in GET: plotPars=plot_chros=8,9,17::labels=8:120000000-123000000:Some+Interesting+Region::plot_gene_symbols=MYCN,TP53,MTAP,CDKN2A,MYC,ERBB2::plot_width=800 * in CMD: --plotPars "plot_chros=8,9,17::labels=8:120000000-123000000:Some Interesting Region::plot_gene_symbols=MYCN,TP53,MTAP,CDKN2A,MYC,ERBB2::plot_width=800"

docs.title: CNV histograms of collations - /services/collationplots
docs.text:
CNV histograms can be generated either (fast) for one or multiple of the "collations" i.e. samples sharing a common code (diagnosis, technnique...) or identifier (cell line id, PMID ...), or as single histogram for the output of a Beacon query.

A complete list of collations can be retrieved through the /services/collations/ endpoint, e.g. /services/collations?datasetIds=progenetix - an option &output=text should provide this as a table instead of Beacon-style JSON response.

Please note that the datasetIds parameter will fall back to the default parameter if not indicated.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
The examples below link to progenetix.org.

  • /services/collationplots/?filters=NCIT:C35562,NCIT:C3709
    • a combination of 2 histograms
  • /services/collationplots?filters=NCIT:C35562,NCIT:C3709&datasetIds=progenetix,cellz
    • a combination of 2 histograms
  • /services/collationplots/?filters=pgx:icdom-85003,pgx:icdom-81703,pgx:icdom-87003,pgx:icdom-87203,pgx:icdom-94003,pgx:icdom-95003,pgx:icdom-81403&plotPars=plot_title=CNV+Comparison::plot_area_height=50::plot_axis_y_max=80::plot_label_y_values=50
    • a collations based example showing the use of some extra parameters such as
      • plot_title
      • plot_area_height
      • plot_axis_y_max & plot_label_y_values

docs.title: CNV sample plots - /services/sampleplots
docs.text:
Sample selection based plotting uses the standard bycon query stack for sample retrieval (i.e. aggregation over the data model) and then generates CNV plots from the found samples, either as clustered individual profiles or as binned frequency plot data (histograms or heatstrips).

CAVE: Sample plots may be time consuming due to the retrieval and plotting of all variants per sample. Therefore, usually a limit (default or via Beacon limit parameter) is being applied.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
• /services/sampleplots?filters=pgx:icdom-95003&plotPars=plot_filter_empty_samples=y::plotGeneSymbols=MYCN::plotType=samplesplot&limit=100
    • this example is based on the histoplot example above, but based on individual sample retrieval and plotting and with some plot modifications:
      • limits the output to 100 samples (limit=100)
      • removes samples w/o CNVs (plot_filter_empty_samples=y)
  • /services/sampleplots?filters=pgx:icdom-95003&plotPars=plotGeneSymbols=MYCN&limit=100&plotType=samplesplot
    • this example gets samples for ICD-O Morphology 95003/3 (a.k.a. pgx:icdom-95003)
    • limits the output to the first 1000 samples (limit=1000)
    • adds a label for the MYCN gene
  • /services/sampleplots?filters=pgx:icdom-95003&plotPars=plotGeneSymbols=MYCN&limit=100
    • this is the same selection and labeling but defaulting to the histoplot option since no plotType parameter is indicated

docs.title: Plot parameters
docs.text:
Plot parameters can be given both in snake_case and in the corresponding camelCase format (plot_area_width or plotAreaWidth). Please see the box above for the concatenation syntax!

A detailed list of plot parameters is provided on this page.

docs.title: bycon Arguments and Parameters
docs.text: The following is a list of arguments and parameters used in the bycon package as well as the byconaut tools.

docs.title: user_name
docs.text: type: string pattern: ^\w+$ cmdFlags: --userName description: faking a user name

docs.title: test_mode
docs.text: type: boolean cmdFlags: -t,--testMode description: test setting, i.e. returning some random documents default: False

docs.title: skip
docs.text: type: integer cmdFlags: --skip description: pages to be skipped default: 0

docs.title: limit
docs.text: type: integer cmdFlags: -l,--limit description: limit number of documents; a value of 0 sets to unlimited default: 200

docs.title: requested_granularity
docs.text: type: string pattern: ^\w+$ description: The requested granularity of the beacon cmdFlags: --requestedGranularity default: record

docs.title: request_entity_path_id
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: --requestEntityPathId description: data entry point, equal to the first REST path element in Beacon

docs.title: requested_schema
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: --requestedSchema description: requested schema, e.g. biosample

docs.title: include_resultset_responses
docs.text:
type: string cmdFlags: --includeResultsetResponses description:

  • include resultset responses, e.g. HIT, MISS
  • kind of a holdover from Beacon pre-v1

docs.title: dataset_ids
docs.text: type: array items: string cmdFlags: -d,--datasetIds description: dataset ids

docs.title: filters
docs.text: type: array items: string cmdFlags: --filters description: prefixed filter values, comma concatenated

docs.title: filter_precision
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: --filterPrecision description: either start or exact (exact being internal default) for matching filter values default: exact

docs.title: filter_logic
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: --filterLogic description: either OR or AND (translated to the MongoDB $and etc.) default: AND

docs.title: include_descendant_terms
docs.text: type: boolean cmdFlags: --includeDescendantTerms description: global treatment of descendant terms default: True

docs.title: assembly_id
docs.text: type: string pattern: ^\w+?[\w\-\.]*?\w*?$ db_key: assembly_id cmdFlags: --assemblyId description: assembly id

docs.title: reference_name
docs.text: type: string db_key: location.sequence_id pattern: ^\w+.*?\w?$ cmdFlags: --referenceName description: chromosome

docs.title: mate_name
docs.text: type: string db_key: location.sequence_id pattern: ^\w+.*?\w?$ cmdFlags: --mateName description: chromosome

docs.title: reference_bases
docs.text: type: string db_key: reference_sequence pattern: ^[ACGTN]+$ cmdFlags: --referenceBases description: reference bases

docs.title: alternate_bases
docs.text: type: string db_key: sequence pattern: ^[ACGTN]+$ cmdFlags: --alternateBases description: alternate bases

docs.title: variant_type
docs.text: type: string db_key: variant_state.id pattern: ^\w+[\w \-\:]\w+?$ cmdFlags: --variantType description: variant type, e.g. DUP

docs.title: start
docs.text: type: array db_key: location.start items: - type: integer - pattern: ^\d+?$ cmdFlags: --start description: genomic start position

docs.title: end
docs.text: type: array db_key: location.end items: - type: integer - pattern: ^\d+?$ cmdFlags: --end description: genomic end position

docs.title: variant_min_length
docs.text: type: integer db_key: info.var_length pattern: ^\d+?$ cmdFlags: --variantMinLength description: variantMinLength: The minimal variant length in bases for e.g. CNV queries.

docs.title: variant_max_length
docs.text: type: integer db_key: info.var_length pattern: ^\d+?$ cmdFlags: --variantMaxLength description: variantMaxLength: The maximum variant length in bases for e.g. CNV queries.

docs.title: gene_id
docs.text: type: string db_key: None pattern: ^\w+?(\w+?(\-\w+?)?)?$ cmdFlags: --geneId description: gene id

docs.title: aminoacid_change
docs.text: type: string db_key: molecular_attributes.aminoacid_changes pattern: ^\w+?$ examples: V600E,M734V,G244A cmdFlags: --aminoacidChange description: Aminoacid alteration in 1 letter format

docs.title: genomic_allele_short_form
docs.text: type: string db_key: identifiers.genomicHGVS_id pattern: ^\w+.*\w$ examples: NC_000017.11:g.7674232C>G cmdFlags: --genomicAlleleShortForm description: Genomic HGVSId descriptor

docs.title: variant_internal_id
docs.text: type: string db_key: variant_internal_id pattern: ^\w[\w\:\-\,]+?\w$ examples: 11:52900000-134452384:EFO_0030067 cmdFlags: --variantInternalId description: An id value used for all variant instances of the same composition; a kind of digest

docs.title: accessid
docs.text: type: string db_key: id pattern: ^\w[\w\-]+?\w$ examples: b59857bc-0c4a-4ac8-804b-6596c6566494 cmdFlags: --accessid description: An accessid for retrieving handovers etc.

docs.title: file_id
docs.text: type: string pattern: ^\w[\w\-]+?\w$ examples: 90e19951-1443-4fa8-8e0b-6b5d8c5e45cc cmdFlags: --fileId description: A file id e.g. as generated by the uploader service

docs.title: id
docs.text: type: string db_key: id pattern: ^\w[\w\:\-\,]+?\w$ examples: pgxvar-5bab576a727983b2e00b8d32,pgxind-kftx25eh cmdFlags: --id description: An id; this parameter only makes sense for specific REST entry types

docs.title: ids
docs.text: type: array items: string cmdFlags: --ids description: One or more ids; this parameter only makes sense for specific REST entry types

docs.title: biosample_ids
docs.text: type: array items: string byc_entity: biosample cmdFlags: --biosampleIds description: biosample ids

docs.title: analysis_ids
docs.text: type: array items: string byc_entity: analysis cmdFlags: --analysisIds description: callset / analysis ids

docs.title: individual_ids
docs.text: type: array items: string byc_entity: individual cmdFlags: --individualIds description: subject ids

docs.title: variant_ids
docs.text: type: array items: string byc_entity: genomicVariant cmdFlags: --variantIds description: variant ids

docs.title: debug_mode
docs.text: type: boolean cmdFlags: --debugMode description: debug setting

docs.title: show_help
docs.text: type: boolean cmdFlags: --showHelp description: specific help display

docs.title: test_mode_count
docs.text: type: integer cmdFlags: --testModeCount description: setting the number of documents reurned in test mode default: 5

docs.title: output
docs.text:
type: string cmdFlags: --output description: For defining a special output format, mostly for byconaut services use. Examples:

  • cnvstats, for analyses, to present some CNV statistics
  • pgxseg, using the .pgxseg variant file format
  • text, for some services to deliver a text table instead of JSON

docs.title: include_handovers
docs.text: type: boolean default: True cmdFlags: --includeHandovers description: only used for web requests & testing

docs.title: only_handovers
docs.text: type: boolean default: False cmdFlags: --onlyHandovers description: only used for web requests & testing

docs.title: method
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: --method description: special method default: None

docs.title: group_by
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: -g,--groupBy description: group parameter e.g. for subset splitting default: text

docs.title: parse
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: -p,--parse description: input value to be parsed

docs.title: mode
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: -m,--mode description: mode, e.g. file type

docs.title: key
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: -k,--key description: some key or word

docs.title: update
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: -u,--update description: update existing records default: False

docs.title: inputfile
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: -i,--inputfile description: a custom file to specify input data, usually tab-delimited with special header

docs.title: outputdir
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: --outputdir description: output directory where supported (cmd line)

docs.title: outputfile
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: -o,--outputfile description: output file where supported (cmd line)

docs.title: randno
docs.text: type: integer cmdFlags: -r,--randno description: random number to limit processing, where supported default: 0

docs.title: min_number
docs.text: type: integer cmdFlags: --minNumber description: minimal number, e.g. for collations, where supported default: 0

docs.title: source
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: -s,--source description: some source label, e.g. analyses

docs.title: delivery_keys
docs.text: type: array items: string cmdFlags: --deliveryKeys description: delivery keys

docs.title: collation_types
docs.text: type: array items: string cmdFlags: --collationTypes description: selected collation types, e.g. "EFO"

docs.title: with_samples
docs.text: type: integer cmdFlags: --withSamples description: only for the collations; number of code_matches...

docs.title: selected_beacons
docs.text: type: array items: string

docs.title: genome_binning
docs.text: type: string default: 1Mb cmdFlags: --genomeBinning description: one of the predefined genome binning keys - default 1Mb

docs.title: cyto_bands
docs.text: type: string db_key: None pattern: ^(?:chro?)?([12]?[\dXY])([pq](?:(?:ter)|(?:cen)|(?:[1-4](?:\d(?:\.\d\d*?)?)?)?))?\-?([pq](?:(?:cen)|(?:ter)|(?:[1-4](?:\d(?:\.\d\d*?)?)?)?))?$ cmdFlags: --cytoBands description: cytobands, e.g. 8q21q24.1

docs.title: chro_bases
docs.text: type: string db_key: None pattern: ^(chro?)?([12]?[\dXY])\:(\d+?)(\-(\d+?))?$ cmdFlags: --chroBases description: only for the cytoband converter ... e.g. 8:0-120000000

docs.title: city
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: -c,--city description: only for the geolocations...

docs.title: geo_latitude
docs.text: type: number cmdFlags: --geoLatitude description: only for the geolocations...

docs.title: geo_longitude
docs.text: type: number cmdFlags: --geoLongitude description: only for the geolocations...

docs.title: geo_distance
docs.text: type: integer cmdFlags: --geoDistance description: only for the geolocations...

docs.title: marker_type
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: --markerType description: marker type, only for the geolocations...

docs.title: plot_pars
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: --plotPars description: plot parameters in form par=value concatenated by ::

docs.title: plot_type
docs.text: type: string cmdFlags: --plotType description: plot type (histoplot, samplesplot, arrayplot - more?)

docs.title: histoplot
docs.text: description: The default option, used to plot histograms of the CNV frequencies per data collection ("collation") or aggregated sample data. data_key: interval_frequencies_bundles data_type: collations

docs.title: histoheatplot
docs.text: description: A "heatmap" style version of the histogram plot, where a single gain/loss frequency result is transformed into a small heat color strip. data_key: interval_frequencies_bundles data_type: collations

docs.title: histosparkplot
docs.text: description: A version of the histogram with predefined parameters for representing a small and unlabeled plot, e.g. for use in hover previews. As in the normal histogram parameters can be overridden. data_key: interval_frequencies_bundles data_type: collations mods: - plot_chro_height: 0 - plot_title_font_size: 0 - plot_area_height: 18 - plot_margins: 0 - plot_axislab_y_width: 0 - plot_grid_stroke: 0 - plot_footer_font_size: 0 - plot_width: 480 - plot_area_opacity: 0 - plot_dendrogram_width: 0 - plot_labelcol_width: 0 - plot_axis_y_max: 80 modded: histoplot

docs.title: samplesplot
docs.text: description: A plot of the called CNV segments per sample, with the samples ordered by their clustering (i.e. similarity of binned CNV data). data_key: callsets_variants_bundles data_type: samples

docs.title: plot_id
docs.text: default: genomeplot

docs.title: plot_title
docs.text: description: title above the plot

docs.title: plot_group_by
docs.text: description: group samples in histograms by a filter type (NCIT, PMID...) default: ``

docs.title: plot_filter_empty_samples
docs.text: description: By setting to true samples w/o data can be removed e.g. from sample plots type: boolean default: False

docs.title: force_empty_plot
docs.text: description: By setting to true a plot strip will be forced even if there are no CNV samples type: boolean default: False

docs.title: plot_cluster_results
docs.text: description: By setting to false clustering can be suppressed type: boolean default: True

docs.title: plot_samples_cluster_type
docs.text:
description: Selection of which measurees are used to generate the clustering matrix

  • intcoverage uses the ~2x3k (gain, loss) 1MB intervals
  • chrostats only uses the CNV coverage per chromosomal arm (separately for gains and losses) default: intcoverage oneOf: chrostats,intcoverage

docs.title: plot_cluster_metric
docs.text: default: ward oneOf: average,centroid,complete,median,single,ward,weighted

docs.title: plot_dup_color
docs.text: default: #FFC633

docs.title: plot_amp_color
docs.text: default: #FF6600

docs.title: plot_del_color
docs.text: default: #33A0FF

docs.title: plot_homodel_color
docs.text: default: #0033CC

docs.title: plot_loh_color
docs.text: default: #0066FF

docs.title: plot_snv_color
docs.text: default: #FF3300

docs.title: plot_width
docs.text:
description:

  • width of the plot image, in px
  • the plot area width is determined through
    • plot_width - 2 *plot_margins - plot_labelcol_width - plot_axislab_y_width - plot_dendrogram_width type: integer default: 1024

docs.title: plot_area_height
docs.text: description: height of the plot area (applies only to histogram plots) type: integer default: 100

docs.title: plot_axis_y_max
docs.text:
description:

  • frequency value the maximum of the Y-axis corresponds to
  • use this to e.g. spread values if a max. of less than 100 is expected type: integer default: 100

docs.title: plot_samplestrip_height
docs.text: description: height of a single sample strip type: integer default: 12

docs.title: plot_margins
docs.text: description: outer plot margins, in px type: integer default: 25

docs.title: plot_labelcol_width
docs.text:
description:

  • width of the space for left text labels (e.g. sample ids, collation labels)

  • defaults to 0 when only one item type: integer default: 220

docs.title: plot_axislab_y_width
docs.text: description: width of the space for histogram percentage markers type: integer default: 30

docs.title: plot_dendrogram_width
docs.text:
description:

  • width of the cluster tree
  • defaults to 0 when no clustering is being performed type: integer default: 50

docs.title: plot_dendrogram_color
docs.text: description: color of the cluster tree stroke default: #333333

docs.title: plot_dendrogram_stroke
docs.text: description: thickness of the cluster tree stroke type: number default: 0.5

docs.title: plot_chro_height
docs.text: description: height (well, width...) of the chromosomes in the ideogram strip type: integer default: 14

docs.title: plot_region_gap_width
docs.text: type: integer default: 3

docs.title: plot_canvas_color
docs.text: description: color of the document background default: #ffffff

docs.title: plot_area_color
docs.text: description: color of the plot area background default: #eef6ff

docs.title: plot_area_opacity
docs.text: description: opacity of the plot background type: number default: 0.8

docs.title: plot_heat_intensity
docs.text: description: factor for frequency heatmap value brightness type: number default: 1

docs.title: plot_grid_stroke
docs.text: type: integer default: 1

docs.title: plot_grid_color
docs.text: description: color of grid lines default: #c0e3ee

docs.title: plot_grid_opacity
docs.text: type: float default: 0.8

docs.title: plot_font_color
docs.text: default: #000000

docs.title: plot_font_size
docs.text: description: font size, in px type: integer default: 10

docs.title: plot_title_font_size
docs.text: description: title font size, in px type: integer default: 16

docs.title: plot_labelcol_font_size
docs.text: description: label font size (left column), in px type: integer default: 12

docs.title: plot_label_y_font_size
docs.text: description: font size for Y-axis labels (percents ...) type: integer default: 8

docs.title: plot_label_y_font_color
docs.text: description: font color for Y-axis labels (percents ...) default: #666666

docs.title: plot_label_y_values
docs.text: type: array items: integer default: 25,50,75

docs.title: plot_label_y_unit
docs.text: type: string default: %

docs.title: plot_probe_y_factor
docs.text: description: relative y-scaling of the probes in array-/probeplots type: integer default: 1

docs.title: plot_probe_label_y_values
docs.text: type: array items: number default: 1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9

docs.title: plot_probedot_size
docs.text: type: integer default: 1

docs.title: plot_probedot_opacity
docs.text: type: integer default: 222

docs.title: plot_region_labels
docs.text:
description:

  • placeholder for markers / labels in the
  • format is 8:120000000-124000000:Region+of+Interest
  • comma-concatenation for multiple values
  • label is optional type: array

docs.title: plot_regionlabel_color
docs.text: default: #ddceff

docs.title: plot_gene_symbols
docs.text:
description:

  • label a gene's position by its symbol (CDKN2A, MYC, ERBB2...)
  • comma-concatenation for multiple values type: array

docs.title: plot_cytoregion_labels
docs.text:
description:

  • label a cytoband's position (8q24, 1p12p11, 17q...)
  • comma-concatenation for multiple values type: array

docs.title: plot_cytoregion_color
docs.text: default: #ffe3ee

docs.title: plot_marker_font_color
docs.text: description: font color for gene and region markers default: #dd3333

docs.title: plot_marker_font_size
docs.text: type: integer default: 10

docs.title: plot_marker_label_padding
docs.text: description: text padding of markers versus background/box type: integer default: 4

docs.title: plot_marker_lane_padding
docs.text: type: integer default: 2

docs.title: plot_footer_font_size
docs.text: type: integer default: 10

docs.title: plot_footer_font_color
docs.text: default: #999999
